mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously as long as the rate trajectory is downward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% provides room for easing
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a 2-month window, signaling labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors reduce exposure to cyclical assets.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.3% is near trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite UNRATE > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both decline month-over-month simultaneously, indicating weakening aggregate demand that compresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month in same calendar month
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 726283M, HOUST at 1428K units
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of co-decline instances, or the joint signal produces lower Rank IC than either series independently over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines for two consecutive months, signaling supply-side weakness that reduces corporate profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 103.9867, recent trend available
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of 2-month decline instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized rate while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 3% year-over-year, creating a goldilocks regime of strong growth without overheating that produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year change < 3%
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 45% of goldilocks regime instances, or the interaction signal produces lower Rank IC than either series independently.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100K new jobs per month, signaling labor market cooling that reduces consumer spending power and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change falls below 100K jobs
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of sub-100K job creation months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 3.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 4.5%, indicating real rates are negative and monetary policy is accommodative, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors seek inflation hedges.
regime_hint: negative real rates with accommodative policy
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of negative real rate instances, or the interaction produces lower Rank IC than either series independently over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1.2 million annualized units while retail sales (RSAFS) remain flat or decline, indicating both residential and consumer demand are weakening simultaneously, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings estimates compress.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1200K annualized units AND RSAFS month-over-month change ≤ 0%
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of co-weakness instances, or the joint signal produces lower Rank IC than either series independently over any 3-year rolling window.
